description: Household servants and those who maintain estates.
jobs: Gatekeeper
A broad-shouldered sentinel challenging visitors at the castle gate before granting entry.
Dairy Maid
A cream-splashed worker milking cows at dawn and pressing curds into wheels of cheese.
Stable Hand
A straw-flecked laborer mucking stalls and brushing down horses each evening.
Groom
A gentle-handed attendant feeding, watering, and exercising horses before they are saddled.
Ostler
A lantern-carrying stableman taking travelers' horses at the inn door for the night.
Baker
A flour-dusted early riser pulling hot loaves from a clay oven before dawn.
Butcher
A blood-aproned worker slaughtering livestock and carving carcasses into cuts for sale.
Cook
A ladle-wielding figure stirring pots over the hearth, seasoning stews for the household.
Chef
A commanding presence planning elaborate menus and plating each dish with exacting care.
Kitchen Boy
A soot-smudged youth chopping vegetables, turning the spit, and scrubbing pots on command.
Scullery Maid
Red-knuckled and tireless, scrubbing grease from pots and mopping the kitchen floor.
Gardener
A dirt-kneed tender of hedges and flower beds, keeping the manor grounds in order.
Stable Master
A firm-voiced overseer managing every groom, stall, and horse in the stables.
Butler
A silver-polishing steward running the household staff and serving at the lord's table.
Housekeeper
A key-laden matron supervising maids and ensuring the household runs without fault.
Valet
A sharp-pressed attendant laying out the master's clothes and tending to personal matters.
Lady's Maid
A deft-fingered companion dressing hair, lacing gowns, and guarding private affairs.
Footman
A livery-clad servant opening doors, announcing guests, and serving meals with poise.
Maid
A dust-cloth-in-hand worker sweeping corridors and emptying chamber pots through the house.
Chambermaid
A linen-armed attendant making beds and keeping the sleeping quarters spotless.
Parlor Maid
A silver-polishing presence tidying reception rooms and laying the tea table.
Kitchen Maid
A steam-flushed helper peeling, chopping, and stirring alongside the cook from dawn.
Laundry Maid
A lye-reddened washer boiling linens in great tubs and pressing them flat with hot irons.
Nurse Maid
A soft-voiced caretaker feeding, bathing, and watching over young children day and night.
Wet Nurse
A milk-giving woman breastfeeding another's infant, often living within the household.
Governess
A prim instructor teaching children reading, manners, and arithmetic in the schoolroom.
Companion
A quiet presence reading aloud and providing daily company to a lonely noble.
Doorkeeper
A steadfast figure stationed at the entrance, admitting the expected and turning away strangers.
Groundskeeper
A weather-beaten worker mowing lawns, trimming hedges, and mending fences across the estate.
Master of Horse
A commanding equestrian overseeing every horse, stable, and groom in the lord's service.
Stable Boy
A hay-dusted youth hauling feed buckets and shoveling manure at first light.
Cup Bearer
A watchful server pouring wine into the lord's cup and tasting it first for poison.
Pantler
A provisions-counting steward managing the pantry and rationing stored bread and goods.
Ewerer
A basin-carrying attendant offering warm water and linen towels for nobles to wash.
Physician
A robe-wearing examiner prescribing herbal remedies and lancing infected wounds.
Surgeon
A blood-stained cutter setting bones, stitching wounds, and removing diseased tissue.
Midwife
A steady-handed deliverer of newborns, coaching mothers through labor by firelight.
Barber Surgeon
A razor-wielding dual-trade worker shaving chins by morning and letting blood by afternoon.
Dentist
A plier-gripping practitioner peering into open mouths to pull rotten teeth and pack cavities.
Beard Trimmer
A scissors-steady specialist clipping and shaping facial hair with practiced care.
Tooth Puller
A forceps-armed yanker of damaged teeth from screaming patients at the market stall.
Bone Setter
Strong hands feeling along a broken limb, pulling it straight, and splinting it with wood.
Bloodletter
A lancet-wielding practitioner opening veins and collecting blood into a bowl for treatment.
Feast Master
A detail-obsessed planner arranging seating, courses, and entertainment for grand banquets.
Banquet Coordinator
A schedule-juggling organizer timing each course and arranging flowers for formal dinners.
Wine Steward
A cellar-dwelling selector pairing wines with each course at the lord's table.
Sommelier
A vintage-tasting expert advising on pairings and curating the estate's wine collection.
Hostess
A warmly greeting presence assigning rooms and ensuring every visitor feels welcome.
Hospitality Manager
A logistics-minded coordinator of lodging, meals, and entertainment for large gatherings.
Confidant
A tight-lipped listener trusted with a lord's secrets, sworn never to repeat a word.
Personal Assistant
A quick-footed aide running errands and organizing schedules through a busy day.
Caregiver
A gentle-handed tender of the bedridden, changing linens and spooning warm broth.
Nursemaid
A cradle-rocking soother telling bedtime stories and calming crying children through the night.
Therapist
A patient listener offering guidance to ease anxiety and grief in troubled souls.
Wound Dresser
A vinegar-dabbing healer cleaning cuts, applying poultices, and wrapping injuries in linen.
Plague Doctor
A beaked-mask figure visiting plague houses and burning herbs to ward off contagion.
Pot Washer
Sand-scoured hands scraping burnt food from iron pots in clouds of greasy steam.
Dish Washer
A soap-slick worker stacking, soaking, and scrubbing plates and cutlery after every meal.
Laundry Worker
A river-stone beater of wet clothing, hanging it to dry on lines in the sun.
Clothes Washer
A washboard-bent scrubber soaking stained garments in lye and rinsing them clean.
